mnemonics.title: IoT Design Steps
mnemonics.content: R.A.F.I.P.D.E. - Requirements, Architecture, Flowchart, Interface, Prototype, Deploy, Evaluate

mnemonics.title: IoT Architecture Layers
mnemonics.content: P.N.M.A.B. - Perception (Sensors), Network (Comms), Middleware (Processing), Application (UI), Business (Analytics)

mnemonics.title: Hardware Selection Factors
mnemonics.content: P.P.C.M.N.C.C. - Power, Processing, Cost, Memory, Network, Compatibility

mnemonics.title: Non-Functional Requirements
mnemonics.content: S.P.R.E.F.S. - Scalability, Performance, Reliability, Efficiency, Flexibility, Security

acronyms.usage: Defines how software components interact in IoT systems
Lightweight protocol for resource-constrained IoT devices
Network model for intermittent connectivity in IoT

acronyms.expansion: Enhanced Data rates for GSM Evolution
acronyms.usage: Cellular connectivity option for IoT devices
Category of IoT connectivity for long-range, low-power communication
Wireless firmware/software updates for IoT devices
Measures to ensure reliable IoT data delivery
Architectural style for IoT web services

visualTips.visualization: Draw as a tower: Bottom = Sensors (Physical), Middle layers = Network + Middleware, Top = Applications, Roof = Business Analytics
Left-to-right flow: Sensor → A/D Converter → Microcontroller → Gateway → Cloud → Analytics → User Interface → Actuation feedback loop
Visualize as layers: Inner circle = Edge (fast, local), Middle ring = Fog (regional), Outer ring = Cloud (slow, distant) - time decreases moving inward

analogies.analogy: Like a translator at an international airport converting languages between travelers (sensors) and the country's system (internet)
Like individual drops of water - alone meaningless, but when collected in a bucket (aggregation) and analyzed, reveals patterns
Like a security guard making instant decisions at a building entrance rather than sending every visitor to headquarters for approval
Choosing between MQTT (newsletter - push to subscribers) and CoAP (library request - pull when needed)
